name: Preflight + ecosystem-schema hardening bundle (NDEBT-021, -023, -024, -017, -018)

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Phase-006 F-056: execute the deferred preflight/ecosystem-schema hardening backlog as one bundle, each item carrying a discriminating test. tools/ecosystem_preflight.py: (021.1) NUL-delimited `git status --porcelain=v1 -z` parsing that preserves the directory-prefix tolerate semantics the 043/044 approved runs depend on; (021.2) required-reference check requires a readable regular file (os.path.isfile + os.access R_OK, no longer os.path.exists which accepts directories) and an unresolved HEAD becomes its own blocking finding; (021.3) --repo-root defaults to None so an explicit '.' under --self-fixture is honored; (021.4) the output dir is pre-cleaned of stale preflight.json/preflight.pending.json/baseline.json so mixed-verdict reruns never contradict; (018.1) a new --tolerate-untracked-prefix additive option tolerates a whole in-flight artifact directory by one declared prefix; (017) a new --ci-run-file wires a caller-supplied CI reference into ci_references; (023) find_repository_revision_inconsistencies() guards same-repo revision consistency in build_baseline_document. schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json (024): a blocking_findings array required when verdict==FAIL. tools/validate.sh (023): C12 gains the same-repo revision-consistency check for the ecosystem_baseline family in BOTH entry points. New fixtures: ecosystem_baseline_neg_inconsistent_revisions.json (023, schema-valid but rule-violating) and a rebuilt preflight_verdict_fail.json on blocking_findings (024). tools/fixtures_self_test.sh: the new fixture + standing CLI git-scratch probes (021.5). ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md 0.1.2 (018.2): the commit-before-gated-invocation rule.
design_notes.operator_design_decisions: The operator directed 'Approved, please proceed' for the FULL bundle after being offered scope-down/pause alternatives, and pre-approved two lighter mechanisms over the feature_list's aspirational phrasing: (017) a PLUGGABLE caller-supplied --ci-run-file (deterministic, offline), NOT a live GitHub Actions API call inside the tool -- the tool stays a deterministic collector and the orchestrator/caller (which already resolves CI runs) supplies the reference; (018.1) an ADDITIVE --tolerate-untracked-prefix option, NOT a scratch-checkout/clean-worktree isolation mode -- it achieves 018(1)'s stated goal (a feature's own in-flight .agent artifacts need not be enumerated file-by-file) with far less risk to the load-bearing 043/044 exact-match dir-collapse semantics. The feature_list 056 acceptance_tests (discriminating test per item; 043/044 preserved; sweeps+e2e green; five exit codes unchanged) are met by these mechanisms.
design_notes.preservation_note: The committed 043/044 approved-exception semantics are load-bearing and preserved: git collapses an entirely-untracked directory to one trailing-slash porcelain entry, and the exact --tolerate-untracked matches that collapsed 'dir/' entry unchanged (E06-b). -z does NOT imply --untracked-files=all, so the collapse is retained (021.1). The exact-match set keeps the historic declared_tolerated_untracked kind; the prefix set uses declared_tolerated_untracked_prefix, so the two are never conflated.
design_notes.consistency_check_scope: NDEBT-023's same-repo revision-consistency rule is expressed as: within repository_references, entries sharing the same explicit `repository` identifier MUST declare the same `revision`. This is false-positive-free (revisions are only compared among entries KNOWN to be the same repo by the explicit key) and is NOT expressible as a clean JSON-Schema constraint (a relational cross-item rule), so it is mechanized in code (the tool's pure function + C12), never as a schema guard -- exactly per the NDEBT-023 remediation ('a schema-level guard only if expressible without false positives'). The tool never produces an inconsistency (self-runs anchor one HEAD), so the build_baseline_document guard is a defensive invariant for future multi-repo extension; the pure function is tested directly (E08).
design_notes.process_honesty: Single-session collapsed-role execution, disclosed-and-permitted by the AGF Orchestrator clause. The tool is NOT CI-covered (only its OUTPUT schemas are, via C12), so the executed scratch probes (E02-E09) are its primary regression surface, now complemented by standing CLI probes in the self-test (021.5). All probes use mktemp -d scratch git repos per methodology/02 Sec 11 (scratch only, absolute-path guard, rm -rf -- with a repo-path refusal); expected-FAIL invocations capture rc explicitly per Sec 10(e)/NDEBT-019. Anti-vacuity (E01) and confinement (E13) anchor to the phase base a5d741d (the F-057 merge).

files_create.purpose: NDEBT-023 negative fixture: two fully-anchored (revision+timestamp) repository_references for the SAME repository at DIFFERENT revisions -- schema-valid but violating same-repo consistency; caught by C12's code-level check.

files_modify.purpose: 021.1/.2/.3/.4 + 018.1 + 017 + 023 tool hardening (NUL parse, readable-regular-file + unresolved-HEAD blocking, --repo-root None, output-dir pre-clean, --tolerate-untracked-prefix, --ci-run-file, find_repository_revision_inconsistencies).
024: add blocking_findings array + allOf 'if verdict==FAIL then required blocking_findings (minItems 1)'.
024: rebuild on blocking_findings (drop the muddled exceptions[{blocking:true}]); stays a C12-positive/valid FAIL document.

023: C12 (both check_c12_ecosystem_fixtures full-sweep and check_c12_target --target) gains the same-repo revision-consistency check for the ecosystem_baseline family, layered after schema validation.
021.5: manifest the new fixture (assert_target C12 FAIL) + standing ecosystem_preflight.py git-scratch CLI probes (untracked-not-tolerated -> FAIL, exact/prefix tolerate polarity).
018.2 (0.1.2): Section 4.1 codifies the commit-before-gated-invocation rule for orchestrator-owned gate-decision tracked-state writes.
0.27.0: NDEBT-021/-023/-024/-017/-018 Open -> Resolved with resolution notes.
[Unreleased] Added/Changed: the preflight + ecosystem-schema hardening bundle entry.

non_goals: No live GitHub Actions API call (or any network call) inside the tool -- NDEBT-017 is satisfied by the pluggable, offline --ci-run-file (operator-approved); the tool stays a deterministic collector.

No scratch-checkout / clean-worktree isolation mode -- NDEBT-018(1) is satisfied by the additive --tolerate-untracked-prefix (operator-approved), which achieves the enumeration-ergonomics goal without risking the 043/044 exact-match semantics.

No schema-level same-repo revision-consistency guard -- the rule is a relational cross-item constraint not cleanly expressible in JSON Schema without false positives (NDEBT-023 remediation); it is mechanized in code.
No change to the five documented exit codes (0/1/2/3/64), the three-verdict enum, the six baseline categories, or the preflight.pending.json withholding behavior.
No change to any committed reconciliation/baseline/audit artifact (AH-3) -- the hardening binds future runs; existing 043/044 dogfood artifacts are untouched.

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (phase base a5d741d = the F-057 merge / PR #35): at the base the tool has no --tolerate-untracked-prefix / --ci-run-file / find_repository_revision_inconsistencies; the schema has no blocking_findings; the neg_inconsistent_revisions fixture is absent; ecosystem/01 is 0.1.1 with no gate-decision-before-invocation rule; and all five NDEBT rows are in DEBT Open.
verification.command:
set -e
t=$(git show a5d741d:tools/ecosystem_preflight.py)
for tok in "tolerate-untracked-prefix" "ci-run-file" "find_repository_revision_inconsistencies"; do
  if printf %s "$t" | grep -Fq "$tok"; then echo "VACUITY: tool already has $tok at base"; exit 1; fi
done
if git show a5d741d:schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json | grep -Fq blocking_findings; then echo "VACUITY: schema already has blocking_findings"; exit 1; fi
if git cat-file -e a5d741d:tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_inconsistent_revisions.json 2>/dev/null; then echo "VACUITY: neg fixture already at base"; exit 1; fi
git show a5d741d:ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md | grep -Fq 'version: 0.1.1'
if git show a5d741d:ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md | grep -Fq "before the corresponding gated"; then echo "VACUITY: doc rule already at base"; exit 1; fi
python3 - <<'PY'
import re, subprocess
txt = subprocess.check_output(['git','show','a5d741d:docs/planning/DEBT.md'], text=True)
sect=None; openrows=set()
for l in txt.splitlines():
    if l.startswith('## Open'): sect='open'
    elif l.startswith('## Resolved'): sect='resolved'
    m=re.match(r'\| (NDEBT-\d+) ', l)
    if m and sect=='open': openrows.add(m.group(1))
for r in ('NDEBT-021','NDEBT-023','NDEBT-024','NDEBT-017','NDEBT-018'):
    assert r in openrows, r + ' not Open at base'
print('all five NDEBT rows Open at base')
PY
echo 'E01 PASS: at base a5d741d none of the five items exist and all five NDEBT rows are Open (anti-vacuity)'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-021.1 NUL-DELIMITED PARSE: an untracked file whose repo-relative path contains a SPACE is tolerated by an exact --tolerate-untracked match (exit 2, pending) -- proving -z raw paths, not C-quoted default porcelain-v1 (which would blockingly mis-match).
verification.command:
set -e
SB=$(mktemp -d); OUT=$(mktemp -d)
case "$SB" in ""|"/"|"$PWD"|"$PWD"/*) echo "unsafe scratch: $SB"; exit 1 ;; esac
trap 'rm -rf -- "$SB" "$OUT"' EXIT
git -C "$SB" init -q
git -C "$SB" config user.email t@example.invalid && git -C "$SB" config user.name tester
mkdir -p "$SB/schema"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json"
git -C "$SB" add -A && git -C "$SB" commit -qm init
TOOL="$PWD/tools/ecosystem_preflight.py"
printf x > "$SB/a b.txt"
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB" --tolerate-untracked "a b.txt"; rc=$?; set -e
test "$rc" -eq 2
grep -Fq '"path": "a b.txt"' "$OUT/preflight.pending.json"
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB" --tolerate-untracked "other.txt"; rc2=$?; set -e
test "$rc2" -eq 1
echo 'E02 PASS: space-containing untracked path matches the exact tolerate-list under -z (exit 2); a non-matching tolerate leaves it blocking (exit 1)'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-021.2 READABLE-REGULAR-FILE + UNRESOLVED-HEAD: a DIRECTORY at a required-schema path is still a blocking finding (os.path.exists accepted it before; os.path.isfile rejects it); and an unborn repo (unresolved HEAD) surfaces its own blocking finding rather than silently anchoring the baseline to 'unknown'.
verification.command:
set -e
SB=$(mktemp -d); OUT=$(mktemp -d)
case "$SB" in ""|"/"|"$PWD"|"$PWD"/*) echo "unsafe scratch: $SB"; exit 1 ;; esac
trap 'rm -rf -- "$SB" "$OUT"' EXIT
git -C "$SB" init -q
git -C "$SB" config user.email t@example.invalid && git -C "$SB" config user.name tester
mkdir -p "$SB/schema"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json"
git -C "$SB" add -A && git -C "$SB" commit -qm init
TOOL="$PWD/tools/ecosystem_preflight.py"
rm -f "$SB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json" && mkdir -p "$SB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"
git -C "$SB" add -A && git -C "$SB" commit -qm dir
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB"; rc=$?; set -e
test "$rc" -eq 1
grep -Fq "required reference missing or unreadable: schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json" "$OUT/preflight.json"
UB=$(mktemp -d); case "$UB" in "$PWD"|"$PWD"/*) exit 1 ;; esac
git -C "$UB" init -q
mkdir -p "$UB/schema" && printf '{}' > "$UB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json" && printf '{}' > "$UB/schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json"
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$UB"; rcu=$?; set -e
rm -rf -- "$UB"
test "$rcu" -eq 1
grep -Fq "HEAD does not resolve" "$OUT/preflight.json"
echo 'E03 PASS: a directory at a required-schema path stays blocking (isfile), and an unresolved HEAD is its own blocking finding'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-021.3 --repo-root DEFAULT None: with --self-fixture AND an explicit --repo-root . the tool inspects the caller's current directory (a scratch repo), not the script's own framework repo -- the explicit value is honored, no longer indistinguishable from the old default='.'.
verification.command:
set -e
SB=$(mktemp -d); OUT=$(mktemp -d)
case "$SB" in ""|"/"|"$PWD"|"$PWD"/*) echo "unsafe scratch: $SB"; exit 1 ;; esac
trap 'rm -rf -- "$SB" "$OUT"' EXIT
git -C "$SB" init -q
git -C "$SB" config user.email t@example.invalid && git -C "$SB" config user.name tester
mkdir -p "$SB/schema"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json"
git -C "$SB" add -A && git -C "$SB" commit -qm init
TOOL="$PWD/tools/ecosystem_preflight.py"
name=$(basename "$SB")
( cd "$SB" && python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --self-fixture --repo-root . )
python3 - "$OUT/baseline.json" "$name" <<'PY'
import json, sys
b = json.load(open(sys.argv[1])); want = sys.argv[2]
got = b['repository_references'][0]['repository']
assert got == want, ('expected scratch repo %r, got %r (self-fixture overrode explicit --repo-root .)' % (want, got))
assert got != 'nizam-framework', 'self-fixture auto-resolved to the framework repo despite explicit --repo-root .'
print('repository_references anchored to the caller CWD:', got)
PY
echo 'E04 PASS: explicit --repo-root . is honored under --self-fixture (default None), inspecting the scratch repo not the framework'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-021.4 OUTPUT-DIR PRE-CLEAN: reusing one --output-dir for a PASS run then a FAIL run leaves NO stale baseline.json from the PASS run alongside the FAIL preflight.json -- the pre-clean removes preflight.json/preflight.pending.json/baseline.json before each run so verdict artifacts never contradict.
verification.command:
set -e
SB=$(mktemp -d); OUT=$(mktemp -d)
case "$SB" in ""|"/"|"$PWD"|"$PWD"/*) echo "unsafe scratch: $SB"; exit 1 ;; esac
trap 'rm -rf -- "$SB" "$OUT"' EXIT
git -C "$SB" init -q
git -C "$SB" config user.email t@example.invalid && git -C "$SB" config user.name tester
mkdir -p "$SB/schema"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json"
git -C "$SB" add -A && git -C "$SB" commit -qm init
TOOL="$PWD/tools/ecosystem_preflight.py"
python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p1 --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB"
test -f "$OUT/baseline.json"
grep -Fq '"verdict": "PASS"' "$OUT/preflight.json"
printf x > "$SB/dirty.txt"
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p2 --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB"; rc=$?; set -e
test "$rc" -eq 1
grep -Fq '"verdict": "FAIL"' "$OUT/preflight.json"
if [ -e "$OUT/baseline.json" ]; then echo "STALE baseline.json survived a FAIL run"; exit 1; fi
echo 'E05 PASS: the FAIL run pre-cleaned the PASS run stale baseline.json; no contradictory leftovers'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-018.1 --tolerate-untracked-prefix + 043/044 PRESERVED: an entirely-untracked directory (git collapses it to one 'sub/' entry) is tolerated by --tolerate-untracked-prefix sub/ (exit 2) AND by the exact --tolerate-untracked sub/ dir-collapse form the committed 043/044 exception sets rely on (exit 2); a non-matching exact file path (exit 1) and no tolerate (exit 1) stay blocking.
verification.command:
set -e
SB=$(mktemp -d); OUT=$(mktemp -d)
case "$SB" in ""|"/"|"$PWD"|"$PWD"/*) echo "unsafe scratch: $SB"; exit 1 ;; esac
trap 'rm -rf -- "$SB" "$OUT"' EXIT
git -C "$SB" init -q
git -C "$SB" config user.email t@example.invalid && git -C "$SB" config user.name tester
mkdir -p "$SB/schema"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json"
git -C "$SB" add -A && git -C "$SB" commit -qm init
TOOL="$PWD/tools/ecosystem_preflight.py"
mkdir -p "$SB/sub" && printf x > "$SB/sub/a.txt" && printf y > "$SB/sub/b.txt"
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB" --tolerate-untracked-prefix "sub/"; ra=$?; set -e
test "$ra" -eq 2
grep -Fq declared_tolerated_untracked_prefix "$OUT/preflight.pending.json"
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB" --tolerate-untracked "sub/"; rb=$?; set -e
test "$rb" -eq 2
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB" --tolerate-untracked "sub/a.txt"; rc=$?; set -e
test "$rc" -eq 1
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB"; rd=$?; set -e
test "$rd" -eq 1
echo 'E06 PASS: prefix-tolerate (2) and exact dir-collapse tolerate (2) both work; non-matching exact (1) and no-tolerate (1) stay blocking -- 043/044 exact semantics preserved'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-017 --ci-run-file: a caller-supplied CI-reference JSON replaces the ci_references KNOWN LIMITATION synthesis with the real anchored reference (revision/timestamp/system from the file); without the flag the default honest KNOWN-LIMITATION note is unchanged.
verification.command:
set -e
SB=$(mktemp -d); OUT=$(mktemp -d)
case "$SB" in ""|"/"|"$PWD"|"$PWD"/*) echo "unsafe scratch: $SB"; exit 1 ;; esac
trap 'rm -rf -- "$SB" "$OUT"' EXIT
git -C "$SB" init -q
git -C "$SB" config user.email t@example.invalid && git -C "$SB" config user.name tester
mkdir -p "$SB/schema"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json"
git -C "$SB" add -A && git -C "$SB" commit -qm init
TOOL="$PWD/tools/ecosystem_preflight.py"
python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p1 --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB"
grep -Fq "KNOWN LIMITATION" "$OUT/baseline.json"
printf '{"revision":"ci-abc123","timestamp":"2026-01-02T03:04:05Z","system":"github-actions","conclusion":"success"}' > "$OUT/ci.json"
python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p2 --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB" --ci-run-file "$OUT/ci.json"
python3 - "$OUT/baseline.json" <<'PY'
import json, sys
b = json.load(open(sys.argv[1])); ci = b['ci_references'][0]
assert ci['revision'] == 'ci-abc123', ci
assert ci['timestamp'] == '2026-01-02T03:04:05Z', ci
assert ci.get('system') == 'github-actions', ci
blob = json.dumps(ci)
assert 'KNOWN LIMITATION' not in blob, 'caller-supplied CI ref should not carry the limitation note'
print('ci_references anchored to the caller-supplied run:', ci['revision'])
PY
echo 'E07 PASS: --ci-run-file anchors ci_references to the caller-supplied run (no KNOWN-LIMITATION note); the default note is unchanged when the flag is absent'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-023 SAME-REPO REVISION CONSISTENCY: the tool-level find_repository_revision_inconsistencies() flags two same-repository entries at different revisions and passes distinct repositories; the new schema-VALID-but-rule-violating fixture is caught as [C12] FAIL via --target while the valid baseline stays [C12] PASS -- so the check is code-level (the schema cannot express it), not schema-level.
verification.command:
set -e
python3 - <<'PY'
import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'tools')
import ecosystem_preflight as ep
bad = ep.find_repository_revision_inconsistencies({'repository_references':[
    {'repository':'r','revision':'aaa','timestamp':'t'},
    {'repository':'r','revision':'bbb','timestamp':'t'}]})
assert bad, 'inconsistency not detected'
ok = ep.find_repository_revision_inconsistencies({'repository_references':[
    {'repository':'r','revision':'aaa','timestamp':'t'},
    {'repository':'r2','revision':'bbb','timestamp':'t'}]})
assert not ok, 'false positive across distinct repositories'
print('tool-level consistency check: flags same-repo divergence, passes distinct repos')
PY
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, jsonschema
schema = json.load(open('schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json'))
doc = json.load(open('tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_inconsistent_revisions.json'))
jsonschema.validate(doc, schema)  # MUST NOT raise: the schema alone accepts it
print('the negative fixture is schema-valid alone -> the gap the code-level check closes')
PY
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_inconsistent_revisions.json 2>&1) || true
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Fq '[C12] FAIL ecosystem-fixtures --target routed: ecosystem_baseline'
out2=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0
printf '%s\n' "$out2" | grep -Fq '[C12] PASS ecosystem-fixtures --target routed: ecosystem_baseline'
echo 'E08 PASS: consistency mechanized at tool + validator level; neg fixture schema-valid-but-caught ([C12] FAIL), valid fixture [C12] PASS'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-024 blocking_findings ALIGNMENT: the schema now REQUIRES blocking_findings when verdict==FAIL (a FAIL doc without it is rejected; with it, valid; a PASS without it stays valid); the rebuilt preflight_verdict_fail.json is C12-positive/valid; and the tool's real FAIL emission carries blocking_findings and validates.
verification.command:
set -e
SB=$(mktemp -d); OUT=$(mktemp -d)
case "$SB" in ""|"/"|"$PWD"|"$PWD"/*) echo "unsafe scratch: $SB"; exit 1 ;; esac
trap 'rm -rf -- "$SB" "$OUT"' EXIT
git -C "$SB" init -q
git -C "$SB" config user.email t@example.invalid && git -C "$SB" config user.name tester
mkdir -p "$SB/schema"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json"
printf '{}' > "$SB/schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json"
git -C "$SB" add -A && git -C "$SB" commit -qm init
TOOL="$PWD/tools/ecosystem_preflight.py"
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, jsonschema
schema = json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json'))
try:
    jsonschema.validate({'verdict':'FAIL','execution_id':'x','generated_at':'t'}, schema)
    raise SystemExit('EXPECTED rejection: FAIL without blocking_findings')
except jsonschema.ValidationError:
    pass
jsonschema.validate({'verdict':'FAIL','execution_id':'x','generated_at':'t','blocking_findings':['b']}, schema)
jsonschema.validate({'verdict':'PASS','execution_id':'x','generated_at':'t'}, schema)
print('schema: FAIL requires blocking_findings; PASS does not')
PY
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_fail.json 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Fq '[C12] PASS ecosystem-fixtures --target routed: preflight_verdict'
python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_fail.json')); assert 'blocking_findings' in d and 'exceptions' not in d, d"
printf x > "$SB/dirty.txt"
set +e; python3 "$TOOL" --execution-id p --output-dir "$OUT" --repo-root "$SB"; rc2=$?; set -e
test "$rc2" -eq 1
python3 - "$OUT/preflight.json" <<'PY'
import json, sys, jsonschema
doc = json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))
assert doc['verdict'] == 'FAIL' and doc['blocking_findings'], doc
jsonschema.validate(doc, json.load(open('schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json')))
print('the tool FAIL emission carries blocking_findings and validates against the real schema')
PY
echo 'E09 PASS: schema/fixture/tool aligned on blocking_findings (FAIL requires it; fixture C12-positive; tool emission validates)'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-018.2 GATE-DECISION-BEFORE-INVOCATION RULE: ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md now codifies, as a document-binding rule, that an orchestrator-owned tracked-state write relevant to a gate decision MUST be committed BEFORE the gated preflight invocation runs (naming the F-043 run_state.json incident).
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq '### 4.1' ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md
grep -Fq 'MUST be committed before the corresponding gated' ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md
grep -Fq 'run_state.json' ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md
grep -Fq 'version: 0.1.2' ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md
echo 'E10 PASS: ecosystem/01 4.1 codifies the commit-before-gated-invocation rule (0.1.2)'

verification.acceptance_test: NDEBT-021.5 STANDING PROBE: an executed untracked-not-tolerated -> FAIL probe (and the tolerate polarity) is wired into tools/fixtures_self_test.sh as a standing git-scratch guard for the otherwise not-CI-covered CLI; E06 executes the same probe inline.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq 'ecosystem_preflight' tools/fixtures_self_test.sh
grep -Fq 'untracked-not-tolerated' tools/fixtures_self_test.sh
grep -Eq '_preflight_cli_probes|preflight CLI' tools/fixtures_self_test.sh
echo 'E11 PASS: the untracked-not-tolerated -> FAIL probe is a standing self-test guard for the CLI'

verification.acceptance_test: REGRESSION + EXIT-CODE STABILITY: validator sweep 13/13, payload 11/11, self-test 47/47 (46 + the new consistency fixture), e2e green (each with explicit rc capture); and the CLI's five documented exit codes 0/1/2/3/64 are unchanged (--help still lists them; a usage error is still 64).
verification.command:
set -e
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY: 13 passed, 0 failed'
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 11 passed, 0 failed'
out=$(bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SELF-TEST OK: 47/47 fixtures accounted for, 0 failed'
bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1
python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --help | grep -Fq '64  Usage error'
set +e; python3 tools/ecosystem_preflight.py --output-dir /tmp/nope 2>/dev/null; u=$?; set -e; test $u -eq 64
echo 'E12 PASS: 13/13, 11/11, 47/47 (rc=0 each); e2e green; the five exit codes 0/1/2/3/64 unchanged'

verification.acceptance_test: DELIVERABLE CONFINEMENT: the non-.agent changes across the branch (base a5d741d..HEAD) are exactly the nine contracted files (tool, schema, two fixtures, validator, self-test, preflight doc, DEBT, CHANGELOG).
verification.command:
set -e
changed=$(git diff --name-only a5d741d..HEAD | grep -v '^\.agent/' | LC_ALL=C sort | tr '\n' ' ')
expected='CHANGELOG.md docs/planning/DEBT.md ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md schema/preflight_verdict.schema.json tools/ecosystem_preflight.py tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_inconsistent_revisions.json tools/fixtures/preflight_verdict_fail.json tools/fixtures_self_test.sh tools/validate.sh '
test "$changed" = "$expected"
echo 'E13 PASS: non-.agent changes are exactly the nine contracted deliverable files'

verification.acceptance_test: DOCS/DEBT SYNCED: DEBT 0.27.0 moves NDEBT-021/-023/-024/-017/-018 to Resolved (none left in Open); CHANGELOG [Unreleased] names the F-056 hardening entry.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq 'version: 0.27.0' docs/planning/DEBT.md
python3 - <<'PY'
import re
sect=None; resolved=set(); openrows=set()
for l in open('docs/planning/DEBT.md'):
    if l.startswith('## Open'): sect='open'
    elif l.startswith('## Resolved'): sect='resolved'
    m=re.match(r'\| (NDEBT-\d+) ', l)
    if m and sect=='resolved': resolved.add(m.group(1))
    if m and sect=='open': openrows.add(m.group(1))
for r in ('NDEBT-021','NDEBT-023','NDEBT-024','NDEBT-017','NDEBT-018'):
    assert r in resolved and r not in openrows, r + ' not Resolved'
print('all five NDEBT rows Resolved, none Open')
PY
sed -n '/## \[Unreleased\]/,/## \[0.7.0\]/p' CHANGELOG.md | grep -Fiq 'preflight'
echo 'E14 PASS: DEBT 0.27.0 (five rows Resolved); CHANGELOG names the F-056 entry'

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured output is in .agent/evidence/056-verify-NN.txt (14 entries), plus the consolidated .agent/evidence/056/verification.txt (14 EXIT:0 markers) and .agent/evidence/056/diff.patch (git diff a5d741d..HEAD). Commands run from the repo root; anti-vacuity (E01) and confinement (E13) anchor to the phase base a5d741d (the F-057 merge). CLI probes use mktemp -d scratch git repos (methodology/02 Sec 11 probe isolation); expected-FAIL invocations capture rc explicitly (Sec 10(e)/NDEBT-019). The tool is not CI-covered, so these executed probes are its primary regression surface, complemented by the standing self-test CLI probes.

approvals.process_note: Operator-approved FULL bundle (directive 'Approved, please proceed' after scope-down/pause was offered), with two pre-approved lighter mechanisms recorded in design_notes.operator_design_decisions (--ci-run-file over a live API; --tolerate-untracked-prefix over scratch-checkout). The 14-entry suite executes live with captured evidence (dogfooding NDEBT-019 rc-capture); QA record .agent/qa/056.json documents the independent re-run.
